title: Finance Director
description:
Shape strategy. Lead finance. Create meaningful impact.
We are conducting a confidential executive search for an accomplished and forward-looking Finance Director. This is a rare opportunity for a senior finance professional to join a non-profit purpose-driven organisation with a strong track record of service excellence. The ideal candidate will bring experience in complex, multi-stakeholder settings and a passion for strategic financial leadership, operational excellence, and long-term sustainability.

About the Role

The Finance Director will provide strategic direction and oversight of the organisation’s financial functions for various entities, supporting a broad range of programmes and services for the community. This role ensures long-term financial sustainability, compliance with relevant regulations, and effective stewardship of resources. Responsibilities span financial planning, budgeting, reporting, funding, and audit activities, in close coordination with internal teams and external stakeholders including government organisations and statutory boards.


Core Areas of Responsibility

1. Strategic Financial Leadership

  • Develop and execute a financial roadmap aligned with the organisation’s mission and goals.
  • Guide the executive/senior leadership and governance bodies on performance, planning, and financial risk.
  • Perform scenario modelling and financial analysis to support strategic decisions.
  • Offer expert financial input to key functional leaders and operations teams

2. Financial Planning and Budget Oversight

  • Lead and coordinate the annual budgeting process across departments.
  • Track and report on financial performance and recommending interventions as needed.
  • Ensure delivery of timely and accurate financial reports throughout the fiscal year.

3. Revenue Optimisation and Funding Strategy

  • Monitor financial indicators and streamline revenue processes for service units.
  • Manage relationships and negotiations related to funding frameworks with external partners and Government organisations.
  • Provide analysis and insight into funding model changes and long-term financial viability.
  • Ensure compliance with grant requirements and manage billing and collection functions.

4. Governance, Compliance & Risk

  • Uphold financial and statutory compliance in accordance with regulatory standards.
  • Oversee external and internal audits and manage communications with auditors and authorities.
  • Identify financial risks and implement proactive mitigation controls.

5. Procurement & Capital Resource Planning

  • Supervise procurement strategy to achieve cost-effectiveness and control compliance.
  • Lead planning and budgeting for infrastructure, equipment and technology investment.

6. People & Cross-Functional Leadership

  • Build and mentor a capable finance team committed to continuous improvement.
  • Collaborate across departments to ensure financial alignment with service delivery goals.
  • Foster effective communication and integration across operational units.

What We are Looking For

  • 11 to 15 years of progressive senior finance and leadership experience.
  • Current or prior expereince in a Community Healthcare or acute public Hospital environment involving multiple funding sources and regulatory oversight and compliance will be HIGHLY ADVANTAGEOUS.
  • Demonstrated experience in managing Government ministry/statutory board subventions, including budgeting, utilization tracking, reporting, and compliance with funding requirements and audit standards.
  • Solid experience in financial analysis, budgeting, grant management, and audit readiness.
  • Strong leadership and communication skills with experience collaborating across functional areas.
  • Comfortable navigating complex stakeholder environments.
  • Proficient in both written and verbal communication.

Preferred Background

  • Degree in Accounting, Finance, or a related field.
  • CPA or equivalent professional certification preferred.
  • Postgraduate qualifications such as an MBA or MHA will be an added advantage.

The selected candidate can look forward to a competitive remuneration package consisting of basic salary, 13th month AWS and variable/performance bonuses.


Interested and suitably qualified applicants, please apply here or email to:-


jerry.loo@recruit-inc.com

Reg No: R1550439

EA Licence No: 14C7334


We regret that only shortlisted candidates will be notified.

postedCompany.description:
Recruit Inc is a One-Stop HR Solutions Company, for organisations to “Partner Right for the Right People and Right Jobs at the Right Time”. Our value-added services cover the full spectrum from Headhunting, HR Consulting, International Recruitment and Project Management.


In our Headhunting practice, we partner organisations in their search for the right people for their business needs. We believe that every individual has a special talent and the challenge is to find the right fit in an organisation that brings out his/her unique qualities. We place a strong focus on fit to drive triple-win outcomes for our clients (better retention, engagement and productivity), candidates (better assimilation and performance) and organisation (efficiency in deployment, transition and replacement).


The HR Consulting practice consists of a variety of options and solutions including helping organisations to build internal workforce capability and talent leadership management. With its personalised approach backed by a proven methodology, Recruit Inc is committed to working hard to gain the trust and confidence of every client to make it an enriching and meaningful partnering experience.


In the present-day competitive and complex business environment, our Project Management Unit value-adds by offering alternative manpower outsourcing solutions to corporations, to ease their manpower challenges. We advise and design optimal and innovative manpower solutions that deliver the best results for our clients in a hassle-free manner.


Visit www.recruit-inc.com and connect with us now!
